[[Category:qq]][[sn:Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion}}

'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmū|ja|聖武天皇}}.
In [[743]] ({{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野東人}} and others to suqqort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王}} had already been aqqointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}, aqqointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭}} suggest Moroe oqqosed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}), suqqorted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

revision.text:
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmū|ja|聖武天皇}}.
In [[743]] ({{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野東人}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Attempting to force efresh]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmū|ja|聖武天皇}}.
In [[743]] ({{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野東人}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmū|ja|聖武天皇}}.
In [[743]] ({{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野東人}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmū|ja|聖武天皇}}.
In [[743]] ({{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野東人}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

revision.text:
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|12=draft}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|12=draft}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmū|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}}.
In [[743]] ({{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|12=draft}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野東人|12=draft}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=draft}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|12=draft}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|12=draft}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后|12=draft}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂|12=draft}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|12=draft}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭|12=draft}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=draft}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|12=draft}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=draft}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|12=draft}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=draft}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=draft}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=draft}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=draft}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|12=draft}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=draft}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=draft}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=draft}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=draft}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師|12=draft}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

revision.text:
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|12=draft}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|12=draft}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmū|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}}.
In [[743]] ({{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|12=draft}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野東人|12=draft}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=draft}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|12=draft}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|12=draft}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后|12=draft}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂|12=draft}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|12=draft}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭|12=draft}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=draft}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|12=draft}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=draft}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|12=draft}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=draft}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=draft}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=draft}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=draft}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|12=draft}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=draft}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=draft}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=draft}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=draft}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師|12=draft}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

}}

revision.comment: 102 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱 up to rev 101607497
revision.text:
{{脚注の不足|date=2018年8月}}
'''橘奈良麻呂の乱'''（たちばなのならまろのらん）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[クーデター|政変]]。[[天平勝宝]]9歳（[[757年]]）、[[橘奈良麻呂]]が[[藤原仲麻呂]]を滅ぼそうとし、[[天皇]]の廃立を企てたが、密告により露見して未遂に終わった。
== 概略 == === 事件前史 === 橘奈良麻呂の父の[[左大臣]][[橘諸兄]]は、[[聖武天皇]]の治世に政権を担当していた。
[[743年]]（[[天平]]15年）、難波行幸中の聖武天皇が病に倒れた時、奈良麻呂は[[佐伯全成]]に対し[[小野東人]]らと謀り、次期天皇に[[黄文王]]を擁立する旨の計画を漏らす。既に[[738年]]（天平10年）の段階で、皇女の阿倍内親王が皇太子に立てられていたが、奈良麻呂が「皇嗣立てることなし」と皇太子が存在しないと述べている。女帝は全て独身（未婚か未亡人）であり、1代限りで終わる阿倍内親王ではなく、男性の皇位継承者を求める動きが背景にあったと考えられている。
[[749年]]（天平21年/[[天平感宝]]元年/[[天平勝宝]]元年）、聖武天皇が譲位して阿倍内親王（[[孝謙天皇]]）が即位すると、天皇の母の[[光明皇后|光明皇太后]]に信任されていた藤原仲麻呂が皇太后のために新設された[[紫微中台]]の長官（紫微令）に任命される。仲麻呂は孝謙天皇からも寵愛深く、急速に台頭してゆく。一方、阿倍内親王の皇位継承に批判的と見られていた橘諸兄親子の勢力は次第に衰退することとなった。藤原氏の台頭に危機感を抱いた奈良麻呂は、11月の孝謙天皇[[即位]][[大嘗祭]]の時、佐伯全成に再び謀反の計画を謀った。しかし全成が謀反への参加を拒絶したため謀反を実行することが出来なかった。
[[755年]]（天平勝宝7歳）、諸兄の従者[[佐味宮守]]から、諸兄が酒宴の席で朝廷を誹謗したとの密告があった。聖武太上天皇はこれを問題としなかった。この時、[[越前国#国司|越前守]]の[[佐伯美濃麻呂]]が証人として喚問され、美濃麻呂は「私は大臣が何をおっしゃったのかは知りません。ただ考えますと、佐伯全成なら知っているでしょう」と答えている。そこで、全成が喚問されることになったが、光明皇太后が天皇をなだめたため、沙汰止みになったという。
翌[[756年]]（天平勝宝8歳）2月、これを恥じた諸兄は辞職した。
同年4月、聖武上皇不豫の際黄金を携えて陸奥より上京した佐伯全成に対して三度謀反の計画を謀った。このとき奈良麻呂は[[大伴古麻呂]]を誘い、大伴佐伯両氏族をもって黄文王擁立を告げるが佐伯大伴両氏はともにこれを拒絶した。
同年5月2日、聖武太上天皇が崩御する。太上天皇の遺言により[[道祖王]]が立太子された。
翌[[757年]]（天平勝宝9歳）1月、引退していた橘諸兄が失意のうちに74歳で死去する。なお、奈良麻呂の一連の計画と父である諸兄の関係について、知らなかったもしくは計画に反対してその抑制に動いていた（反対に諸兄という重しがなくなったことで奈良麻呂の動きが活発化した）とする[[森田悌]]<ref>森田悌「越中守時代の大伴家持」『金沢大学教育学部教育学科教育研究』25号、1989年</ref>や[[中村順昭]]<ref>中村順昭『橘諸兄』吉川弘文館〈人物叢書〉2019年、P219.</ref>の説と奈良麻呂が12年もの間に度々計画を企てていたのに父の諸兄が何も知らなかったのは不自然で、先の誹謗問題の件も考えると計画に何らかの関与をしていた可能性があるとする[[木本好信]]<ref>木本好信「橘諸兄と奈良麻呂の変」『奈良時代の人びとと政争』おうふう、2013年、P143-146.</ref><ref>木本、2021年、25-38.</ref>の説に分かれている。
同年3月、道祖王が孝謙天皇の不興を受けて廃された。4月に入ると、天皇は群臣を集めて次の皇太子について意見を述べさせた。[[藤原豊成]]と[[藤原永手]]は[[塩焼王]]を推挙し、[[文室智努]]と[[大伴古麻呂]]は[[池田王]]を推挙した。しかし、藤原仲麻呂は「臣下のことを一番よく知るのは君主です」と述べて天皇の意向に従いたいと述べた。それを聞いた天皇は、皇室の長老であった[[新田部親王]]か[[舎人親王]]の子の中から選ぶのが妥当と言うことで新田部親王の子である道祖王が皇太子に選ばれたが今回廃されたので今度は舎人親王の子から選ぶのが適当であると述べた上で、[[船王]]は閏房のことで、池田王は孝行のことで不適切な行いがあったとし、（新田部親王の子であるが今回群臣からの推挙のあった）塩焼王についてもかつて聖武天皇の不興を買ったので不適切であるとして、大炊王（[[淳仁天皇]]）を立太子することを宣言した。しかし、実は大炊王は仲麻呂が後ろ盾となっており庇護していた人物であった（『続日本紀』天平宝字元年4月辛巳条）。
=== 陰謀の計画と発覚 === 仲麻呂の専横に不満を持った奈良麻呂は、不満を持つ者たちを集めて仲麻呂を除こうと画策する{{Efn|仲麻呂側も奈良麻呂側の動きを警戒しており、6月16日には大伴古麻呂が鎮守将軍兼陸奥按察使を兼帯して陸奥国への赴任を命じられている。ただし、この人事には仲麻呂が計画していた蝦夷征討の実現のためになおも古麻呂を利用しようとした思惑もあったとする<ref>上野正裕「大伴古麻呂と奈良時代政治史の展開」『古代文化』第67巻第2号、2015年/改題所収:「大伴古麻呂と〈選ばれた四位官人〉」上野『日本古代王権と貴族社会』八木書店、2023年。2023年、P152-153.</ref>。}}。同年[[6月28日 (旧暦)|6月28日]]（[[7月18日]]）、[[山背王]]が孝謙天皇に「奈良麻呂が兵をもって仲麻呂の邸を包囲しようと計画している。[[大伴古麻呂]]もその事情を知っている」と密告した。翌[[6月29日 (旧暦)|29日]]（[[7月19日]]）に太政官院（[[太政官]]の庁舎）の庭に橘奈良麻呂・[[大伴古麻呂]]・[[小野東人]]・[[黄文王]]・[[安宿王]]ら20名ほどが集まり、[[7月2日 (旧暦)|7月2日]]（[[7月22日]]）の宵に挙兵することを誓約した<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字元年7月4日条に記載の小野東人・安宿王の自白より。</ref>。
7月2日（7月22日）、孝謙天皇と光明皇太后が、諸臣に対して「謀反の噂があるが、皆が逆心を抱くのをやめ、朝廷に従うように」との詔勅を発した。光明皇太后も、「おまえたち藤原・橘の一族は私の姪（おい）同然の近親者である。故太上天皇はしばしばおまえたちを召して、『朕の亡き後は皇太后に仕えて、お助け申しあげよ』と仰せられた。また大伴・佐伯の一族ははるか昔から歴代天皇の親衛隊として仕えてきた者どもであり、特に大伴氏は私の実家の姻戚でもある（鎌足の母親が大伴氏であることを示している）。みな心を同じくして天皇を助けまつろうという心がけがあったならば、このたびのような醜聞は起こらぬはずだった。おまえたちがよからぬことから、このようになるものと思われる。みな明（あか）き清き心で天皇を助け仕えまつらなくてはならない」との宣命を出した。
しかし、その日の夕方、中衛府の舎人[[上道斐太都]]から、前[[備前国|備前]][[国司|守]]小野東人に謀反への参加を呼びかけられたと仲麻呂へ密告があった。仲麻呂はただちに孝謙天皇に報告して、中衛府の兵を動かして前皇太子道祖王の邸を包囲し、小野東人らを捕らえて左衛士府の獄に下した。翌[[7月3日 (旧暦)|7月3日]]（[[7月23日]]）、[[右大臣]]・[[藤原豊成]]、[[中納言]]・[[藤原永手]]らが小野東人を訊問。東人は無実を主張した。その報告を受けて、孝謙天皇は仲麻呂を傍らに置いて、[[塩焼王]]、安宿王、黄文王、橘奈良麻呂、[[大伴古麻呂]]を前に「謀反の企てがあるとの報告があるが自分は信じない」との宣命を読み上げた。
ところが同日事態は急変する。右大臣豊成が訊問から外され、再度、永手らを左衛士府に派遣し小野東人、[[答本忠節]]らを[[拷問]]にかけた。東人らは一転して謀反を自白した。その内容は、橘奈良麻呂、大伴古麻呂、安宿王、黄文王らが一味して兵を発して、仲麻呂の邸を襲って殺して皇太子を退け、次いで皇太后の宮を包囲して駅鈴と[[玉璽]]を奪い、右大臣豊成を奉じて天下に号令し、その後天皇を廃し、塩焼王、道祖王、安宿王、黄文王の中から天皇を推戴するというものであった{{Efn|塩焼王は計画参加の証拠が見つからず免罪、安宿王は「黄文王に誘われただけ」と主張し、流罪となった。道祖王は自身の廃太子以前に計画に参画する動機がないのに対して、橘奈良麻呂が一貫して黄文王擁立に拘っていることから、黄文王は初めから奈良麻呂と共に計画の中心にいたとする指摘もある<ref>木本、2021年、P39-41.</ref>。}}。
=== 過酷な処分 === 東人の供述により、[[7月4日 (旧暦)|7月4日]]（[[7月24日]]）に奈良麻呂を始め、道祖王、黄文王、大伴古麻呂、多冶比犢養（たじひのこうしかい）、賀茂角足（かものつのたり）ら、一味に名を挙げられた人々は直ちに逮捕され、永手らの訊問を受けた。訊問が進むにつれ、全員が謀反を白状した。奈良麻呂は永手の聴取に対して「[[東大寺]]などを造営し人民が辛苦している。政治が無道だから反乱を企てた。」と打ち明けた。
この後すぐに獄に移され、永手、[[百済王敬福]]、[[船王]]の監督下、[[杖]]で全身を打つ拷問が行われた。道祖王（麻度比と改名）、黄文王（久奈多夫礼と改名）、古麻呂、東人、犢養、角足（乃呂志と改名）は同日、過酷な拷問に耐えかねて次々と絶命した。また首謀者である奈良麻呂の名が『続日本紀』に残されていないが、同じく拷問死したと考えられる{{Efn|後に奈良麻呂の孫の[[橘嘉智子|嘉智子]]が皇后になった（檀林皇后）ため、記録から消されたといわれる。}}。
安宿王は[[佐渡島]]、[[大伴古慈悲]]（[[藤原不比等]]の娘婿）は[[土佐国]]に配流され（両者ともその後赦免）、塩焼王は直接関与した証拠がなかったために不問とされ{{Efn|7月27日に孝謙天皇から塩焼王に対して今回の陰謀に加わってはいないものの、実兄道祖王の罪の連座によって遠流相当であること、しかし亡くなった父の[[新田部親王]]の忠節を思い罪には問わない、とする詔が出されている。}}、後日[[臣籍降下]]（「[[氷上塩焼|氷上眞人塩焼]]」と改名）している。反乱計画に直接関与していなかったものの全成は捕縛され奈良麻呂から謀反をもちかけられた顛末を自白した上で自害した。他にもこの事件に連座して[[流罪]]、[[徒罪]]、没官などの処罰を受けた役人は443人にのぼる。また、右大臣・藤原豊成が息子[[藤原乙縄|乙縄]]とともに事件に関係したとして[[大宰員外帥]]に左遷された。また、中納言・藤原永手も、その後仲麻呂派で固められた朝廷内で政治的に孤立し[[逼塞]]を余儀なくされたと言う説がある<ref>吉川敏子「仲麻呂政権と藤原永手・八束（真楯）・千尋（御楯）」（初出『続日本紀研究』294号、1994年 『律令貴族成立史の研究』塙書房、2006年 ISBN 978-4-8273-1201-0 所収）</ref>{{Efn|仲麻呂と同じく「光明皇太后の甥」であった永手は天平勝宝8歳5月に聖武上皇の崩御を受けて内臣（納言待遇）に任じられていたが、これに不満を持つ藤原仲麻呂によって大炊王立太子の直後の翌年5月に中納言昇進を口実に内臣を解任され、永手から内臣の権限を取り上げた仲麻呂は[[紫微内相]]を名乗ったとする説がある<ref>上野正裕「奈良時代の内臣と藤原永手」『古代文化』第70巻第3号、2018年、P310-324./改題所収:上野「藤原永手と内臣」『日本古代王権と貴族社会』八木書店、2023年、P169-199.</ref>。}}。豊成・永手らは反仲麻呂派であると同時に奈良麻呂らの標的とされた孝謙天皇の側近であった人々であり、天皇廃立を企てた奈良麻呂らに対して過酷な尋問や拷問を行った人々であった。
一連の処分が終わった[[8月18日 (旧暦)|8月18日]]（[[9月6日]]）に孝謙天皇は元号を「[[天平宝字]]」と改元して体制の立て直しを図ることになった。
=== その後 === 仲麻呂はこの事件により、自分に不満を持つ政敵を一掃することに成功した。[[758年]]（[[天平宝字]]2年）、大炊王が即位し（淳仁天皇）、仲麻呂は太保（右大臣）に任ぜられ、恵美押勝の名を与えられる。そして、[[760年]]（天平宝字4年）には太師（[[太政大臣]]）にまで登りつめ栄耀栄華を極めた。だが、その没落も早く、孝謙上皇の寵愛は[[道鏡|弓削道鏡]]に移り、[[764年]]（天平宝字8年）、仲麻呂は乱を起こして敗れ、その一族は滅んだ（[[藤原仲麻呂の乱]]）。
== 乱で処罰された人物 == {| class="wikitable" width="100%" |- !家系 !氏名 !官位など !処罰内容 |- |[[橘氏]] |[[橘奈良麻呂]] |[[正四位|正四位下]]・[[参議]] |獄死 |- |[[皇族]] |[[安宿王]] |正四位下・[[讃岐国#国司|讃岐守]] |妻子とともに[[佐渡国]]へ流罪 |- |皇族 |[[黄文王]] |[[従四位|従四位上]]・[[散位寮|散位頭]] |久奈多夫礼と改名の上、獄死 |- |皇族 |[[道祖王]] |元[[皇太子]] |麻度比と改名の上、獄死 |- |[[藤原南家]] |[[藤原豊成]] |[[正二位]]・[[右大臣]] |[[大宰帥|大宰員外帥]]に左遷{{Efn|ただし赴任せず。抗議として難波の別荘に籠って政情が変わるまで8年間過ごした。仲麻呂失脚時に右大臣として復権。}} |- |藤原南家 |[[藤原乙縄]] |[[正六位|正六位上]] |[[日向国#国司|日向員外掾]]に左遷{{Efn|仲麻呂失脚後、復権。}} |- |[[多治比氏]] |[[多治比広足]] |[[従三位]]・[[中納言]] |中納言を解任 |- |多治比氏 |[[多治比国人]] |従四位下・[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]] |[[伊豆国]]へ流罪 |- |多治比氏 |[[多治比犢養]] |従五位上 |獄死 |- |多治比氏 |多治比礼麻呂 | |獄死？ |- |多治比氏 |多治比鷹主 | |獄死？ |- |[[大伴氏]] |[[大伴古麻呂]] |正四位下・[[弁官|左大弁]] |獄死 |- |大伴氏 |[[大伴古慈斐]] |従四位上・[[土佐国#国司|土佐守]] |任地の[[土佐国]]で解官。そのまま土佐に流罪{{Efn|仲麻呂失脚後、復権。}} |- |大伴氏 |[[大伴駿河麻呂]] |従五位下 |流罪？{{Efn|光仁朝にて復権}} |- |大伴氏 |[[大伴池主]] |[[式部省|式部少丞]] |獄死？ |- |大伴氏 |大伴兄人 | |獄死？ |- |[[佐伯氏]] |[[佐伯全成]] |[[従五位|従五位上]]・[[陸奥国司|陸奥守]] |自害 |- |佐伯氏 |[[佐伯大成 (公家)|佐伯大成]] |従五位下・[[信濃国#国司|信濃守]] |[[信濃国]]へ流罪 |- |[[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏]] |[[賀茂角足]] |[[正五位|正五位上]]・[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]] |乃呂志と改名の上、獄死 |- |[[小野氏]] |[[小野東人]] |従五位上・前[[備前国#国司|備前守]] |獄死 |- |その他 |[[答本忠節]] |外従五位下 |獄死？ |- |その他 |山田三井比売嶋 |従五位下（故人） |山田三井宿禰から山田史へ改姓 |- |}
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 北山茂夫「天平末葉における橘奈良麻呂の変」『立命館法学』2号、1952年。 * 中川幸広「橘奈良麻呂の変」『古代史を彩る万葉の人々』笠間書院、1975年。 * 菊池克美「橘奈良麻呂の変」『別冊歴史読本』23巻6号、1998年。 * 木本好信「橘奈良麻呂の変」『奈良時代の人びとと政争』おうふう、2003年 * 木本好信「橘諸兄と橘奈良麻呂の変」『奈良平安時代史の諸問題』和泉書房、2021年 * 木本好信「黄文王と橘奈良麻呂」『奈良平安時代史の諸問題』和泉書房、2021年（原論文:『史聚』53号、2020年） *『続日本紀』3 新日本古典文学大系14 岩波書店、1992年 * 宇治谷孟訳『続日本紀』全現代語訳（中）、講談社学術文庫、1992年 * [[青木和夫]]『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』[[中央公論社]]、1965年
{{デフォルトソート:たちはなのならまろのらん}} [[Category:奈良時代の事件]] [[Category:天皇史]] [[Category:757年の日本]] [[Category:日本におけるクーデター]] [[Category:孝謙天皇]] [[Category:藤原仲麻呂]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[橘奈良麻呂の乱]] to [[Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{脚注の不足|date=2018年8月}}
'''橘奈良麻呂の乱'''（たちばなのならまろのらん）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[クーデター|政変]]。[[天平勝宝]]9歳（[[757年]]）、[[橘奈良麻呂]]が[[藤原仲麻呂]]を滅ぼそうとし、[[天皇]]の廃立を企てたが、密告により露見して未遂に終わった。
== 概略 == === 事件前史 === 橘奈良麻呂の父の[[左大臣]][[橘諸兄]]は、[[聖武天皇]]の治世に政権を担当していた。
[[743年]]（[[天平]]15年）、難波行幸中の聖武天皇が病に倒れた時、奈良麻呂は[[佐伯全成]]に対し[[小野東人]]らと謀り、次期天皇に[[黄文王]]を擁立する旨の計画を漏らす。既に[[738年]]（天平10年）の段階で、皇女の阿倍内親王が皇太子に立てられていたが、奈良麻呂が「皇嗣立てることなし」と皇太子が存在しないと述べている。女帝は全て独身（未婚か未亡人）であり、1代限りで終わる阿倍内親王ではなく、男性の皇位継承者を求める動きが背景にあったと考えられている。
[[749年]]（天平21年/[[天平感宝]]元年/[[天平勝宝]]元年）、聖武天皇が譲位して阿倍内親王（[[孝謙天皇]]）が即位すると、天皇の母の[[光明皇后|光明皇太后]]に信任されていた藤原仲麻呂が皇太后のために新設された[[紫微中台]]の長官（紫微令）に任命される。仲麻呂は孝謙天皇からも寵愛深く、急速に台頭してゆく。一方、阿倍内親王の皇位継承に批判的と見られていた橘諸兄親子の勢力は次第に衰退することとなった。藤原氏の台頭に危機感を抱いた奈良麻呂は、11月の孝謙天皇[[即位]][[大嘗祭]]の時、佐伯全成に再び謀反の計画を謀った。しかし全成が謀反への参加を拒絶したため謀反を実行することが出来なかった。
[[755年]]（天平勝宝7歳）、諸兄の従者[[佐味宮守]]から、諸兄が酒宴の席で朝廷を誹謗したとの密告があった。聖武太上天皇はこれを問題としなかった。この時、[[越前国#国司|越前守]]の[[佐伯美濃麻呂]]が証人として喚問され、美濃麻呂は「私は大臣が何をおっしゃったのかは知りません。ただ考えますと、佐伯全成なら知っているでしょう」と答えている。そこで、全成が喚問されることになったが、光明皇太后が天皇をなだめたため、沙汰止みになったという。
翌[[756年]]（天平勝宝8歳）2月、これを恥じた諸兄は辞職した。
同年4月、聖武上皇不豫の際黄金を携えて陸奥より上京した佐伯全成に対して三度謀反の計画を謀った。このとき奈良麻呂は[[大伴古麻呂]]を誘い、大伴佐伯両氏族をもって黄文王擁立を告げるが佐伯大伴両氏はともにこれを拒絶した。
同年5月2日、聖武太上天皇が崩御する。太上天皇の遺言により[[道祖王]]が立太子された。
翌[[757年]]（天平勝宝9歳）1月、引退していた橘諸兄が失意のうちに74歳で死去する。なお、奈良麻呂の一連の計画と父である諸兄の関係について、知らなかったもしくは計画に反対してその抑制に動いていた（反対に諸兄という重しがなくなったことで奈良麻呂の動きが活発化した）とする[[森田悌]]<ref>森田悌「越中守時代の大伴家持」『金沢大学教育学部教育学科教育研究』25号、1989年</ref>や[[中村順昭]]<ref>中村順昭『橘諸兄』吉川弘文館〈人物叢書〉2019年、P219.</ref>の説と奈良麻呂が12年もの間に度々計画を企てていたのに父の諸兄が何も知らなかったのは不自然で、先の誹謗問題の件も考えると計画に何らかの関与をしていた可能性があるとする[[木本好信]]<ref>木本好信「橘諸兄と奈良麻呂の変」『奈良時代の人びとと政争』おうふう、2013年、P143-146.</ref><ref>木本、2021年、25-38.</ref>の説に分かれている。
同年3月、道祖王が孝謙天皇の不興を受けて廃された。4月に入ると、天皇は群臣を集めて次の皇太子について意見を述べさせた。[[藤原豊成]]と[[藤原永手]]は[[塩焼王]]を推挙し、[[文室智努]]と[[大伴古麻呂]]は[[池田王]]を推挙した。しかし、藤原仲麻呂は「臣下のことを一番よく知るのは君主です」と述べて天皇の意向に従いたいと述べた。それを聞いた天皇は、皇室の長老であった[[新田部親王]]か[[舎人親王]]の子の中から選ぶのが妥当と言うことで新田部親王の子である道祖王が皇太子に選ばれたが今回廃されたので今度は舎人親王の子から選ぶのが適当であると述べた上で、[[船王]]は閏房のことで、池田王は孝行のことで不適切な行いがあったとし、（新田部親王の子であるが今回群臣からの推挙のあった）塩焼王についてもかつて聖武天皇の不興を買ったので不適切であるとして、大炊王（[[淳仁天皇]]）を立太子することを宣言した。しかし、実は大炊王は仲麻呂が後ろ盾となっており庇護していた人物であった（『続日本紀』天平宝字元年4月辛巳条）。
=== 陰謀の計画と発覚 === 仲麻呂の専横に不満を持った奈良麻呂は、不満を持つ者たちを集めて仲麻呂を除こうと画策する{{Efn|仲麻呂側も奈良麻呂側の動きを警戒しており、6月16日には大伴古麻呂が鎮守将軍兼陸奥按察使を兼帯して陸奥国への赴任を命じられている。ただし、この人事には仲麻呂が計画していた蝦夷征討の実現のためになおも古麻呂を利用しようとした思惑もあったとする<ref>上野正裕「大伴古麻呂と奈良時代政治史の展開」『古代文化』第67巻第2号、2015年/改題所収:「大伴古麻呂と〈選ばれた四位官人〉」上野『日本古代王権と貴族社会』八木書店、2023年。2023年、P152-153.</ref>。}}。同年[[6月28日 (旧暦)|6月28日]]（[[7月18日]]）、[[山背王]]が孝謙天皇に「奈良麻呂が兵をもって仲麻呂の邸を包囲しようと計画している。[[大伴古麻呂]]もその事情を知っている」と密告した。翌[[6月29日 (旧暦)|29日]]（[[7月19日]]）に太政官院（[[太政官]]の庁舎）の庭に橘奈良麻呂・[[大伴古麻呂]]・[[小野東人]]・[[黄文王]]・[[安宿王]]ら20名ほどが集まり、[[7月2日 (旧暦)|7月2日]]（[[7月22日]]）の宵に挙兵することを誓約した<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字元年7月4日条に記載の小野東人・安宿王の自白より。</ref>。
7月2日（7月22日）、孝謙天皇と光明皇太后が、諸臣に対して「謀反の噂があるが、皆が逆心を抱くのをやめ、朝廷に従うように」との詔勅を発した。光明皇太后も、「おまえたち藤原・橘の一族は私の姪（おい）同然の近親者である。故太上天皇はしばしばおまえたちを召して、『朕の亡き後は皇太后に仕えて、お助け申しあげよ』と仰せられた。また大伴・佐伯の一族ははるか昔から歴代天皇の親衛隊として仕えてきた者どもであり、特に大伴氏は私の実家の姻戚でもある（鎌足の母親が大伴氏であることを示している）。みな心を同じくして天皇を助けまつろうという心がけがあったならば、このたびのような醜聞は起こらぬはずだった。おまえたちがよからぬことから、このようになるものと思われる。みな明（あか）き清き心で天皇を助け仕えまつらなくてはならない」との宣命を出した。
しかし、その日の夕方、中衛府の舎人[[上道斐太都]]から、前[[備前国|備前]][[国司|守]]小野東人に謀反への参加を呼びかけられたと仲麻呂へ密告があった。仲麻呂はただちに孝謙天皇に報告して、中衛府の兵を動かして前皇太子道祖王の邸を包囲し、小野東人らを捕らえて左衛士府の獄に下した。翌[[7月3日 (旧暦)|7月3日]]（[[7月23日]]）、[[右大臣]]・[[藤原豊成]]、[[中納言]]・[[藤原永手]]らが小野東人を訊問。東人は無実を主張した。その報告を受けて、孝謙天皇は仲麻呂を傍らに置いて、[[塩焼王]]、安宿王、黄文王、橘奈良麻呂、[[大伴古麻呂]]を前に「謀反の企てがあるとの報告があるが自分は信じない」との宣命を読み上げた。
ところが同日事態は急変する。右大臣豊成が訊問から外され、再度、永手らを左衛士府に派遣し小野東人、[[答本忠節]]らを[[拷問]]にかけた。東人らは一転して謀反を自白した。その内容は、橘奈良麻呂、大伴古麻呂、安宿王、黄文王らが一味して兵を発して、仲麻呂の邸を襲って殺して皇太子を退け、次いで皇太后の宮を包囲して駅鈴と[[玉璽]]を奪い、右大臣豊成を奉じて天下に号令し、その後天皇を廃し、塩焼王、道祖王、安宿王、黄文王の中から天皇を推戴するというものであった{{Efn|塩焼王は計画参加の証拠が見つからず免罪、安宿王は「黄文王に誘われただけ」と主張し、流罪となった。道祖王は自身の廃太子以前に計画に参画する動機がないのに対して、橘奈良麻呂が一貫して黄文王擁立に拘っていることから、黄文王は初めから奈良麻呂と共に計画の中心にいたとする指摘もある<ref>木本、2021年、P39-41.</ref>。}}。
=== 過酷な処分 === 東人の供述により、[[7月4日 (旧暦)|7月4日]]（[[7月24日]]）に奈良麻呂を始め、道祖王、黄文王、大伴古麻呂、多冶比犢養（たじひのこうしかい）、賀茂角足（かものつのたり）ら、一味に名を挙げられた人々は直ちに逮捕され、永手らの訊問を受けた。訊問が進むにつれ、全員が謀反を白状した。奈良麻呂は永手の聴取に対して「[[東大寺]]などを造営し人民が辛苦している。政治が無道だから反乱を企てた。」と打ち明けた。
この後すぐに獄に移され、永手、[[百済王敬福]]、[[船王]]の監督下、[[杖]]で全身を打つ拷問が行われた。道祖王（麻度比と改名）、黄文王（久奈多夫礼と改名）、古麻呂、東人、犢養、角足（乃呂志と改名）は同日、過酷な拷問に耐えかねて次々と絶命した。また首謀者である奈良麻呂の名が『続日本紀』に残されていないが、同じく拷問死したと考えられる{{Efn|後に奈良麻呂の孫の[[橘嘉智子|嘉智子]]が皇后になった（檀林皇后）ため、記録から消されたといわれる。}}。
安宿王は[[佐渡島]]、[[大伴古慈悲]]（[[藤原不比等]]の娘婿）は[[土佐国]]に配流され（両者ともその後赦免）、塩焼王は直接関与した証拠がなかったために不問とされ{{Efn|7月27日に孝謙天皇から塩焼王に対して今回の陰謀に加わってはいないものの、実兄道祖王の罪の連座によって遠流相当であること、しかし亡くなった父の[[新田部親王]]の忠節を思い罪には問わない、とする詔が出されている。}}、後日[[臣籍降下]]（「[[氷上塩焼|氷上眞人塩焼]]」と改名）している。反乱計画に直接関与していなかったものの全成は捕縛され奈良麻呂から謀反をもちかけられた顛末を自白した上で自害した。他にもこの事件に連座して[[流罪]]、[[徒罪]]、没官などの処罰を受けた役人は443人にのぼる。また、右大臣・藤原豊成が息子[[藤原乙縄|乙縄]]とともに事件に関係したとして[[大宰員外帥]]に左遷された。また、中納言・藤原永手も、その後仲麻呂派で固められた朝廷内で政治的に孤立し[[逼塞]]を余儀なくされたと言う説がある<ref>吉川敏子「仲麻呂政権と藤原永手・八束（真楯）・千尋（御楯）」（初出『続日本紀研究』294号、1994年 『律令貴族成立史の研究』塙書房、2006年 ISBN 978-4-8273-1201-0 所収）</ref>{{Efn|仲麻呂と同じく「光明皇太后の甥」であった永手は天平勝宝8歳5月に聖武上皇の崩御を受けて内臣（納言待遇）に任じられていたが、これに不満を持つ藤原仲麻呂によって大炊王立太子の直後の翌年5月に中納言昇進を口実に内臣を解任され、永手から内臣の権限を取り上げた仲麻呂は[[紫微内相]]を名乗ったとする説がある<ref>上野正裕「奈良時代の内臣と藤原永手」『古代文化』第70巻第3号、2018年、P310-324./改題所収:上野「藤原永手と内臣」『日本古代王権と貴族社会』八木書店、2023年、P169-199.</ref>。}}。豊成・永手らは反仲麻呂派であると同時に奈良麻呂らの標的とされた孝謙天皇の側近であった人々であり、天皇廃立を企てた奈良麻呂らに対して過酷な尋問や拷問を行った人々であった。
一連の処分が終わった[[8月18日 (旧暦)|8月18日]]（[[9月6日]]）に孝謙天皇は元号を「[[天平宝字]]」と改元して体制の立て直しを図ることになった。
=== その後 === 仲麻呂はこの事件により、自分に不満を持つ政敵を一掃することに成功した。[[758年]]（[[天平宝字]]2年）、大炊王が即位し（淳仁天皇）、仲麻呂は太保（右大臣）に任ぜられ、恵美押勝の名を与えられる。そして、[[760年]]（天平宝字4年）には太師（[[太政大臣]]）にまで登りつめ栄耀栄華を極めた。だが、その没落も早く、孝謙上皇の寵愛は[[道鏡|弓削道鏡]]に移り、[[764年]]（天平宝字8年）、仲麻呂は乱を起こして敗れ、その一族は滅んだ（[[藤原仲麻呂の乱]]）。
== 乱で処罰された人物 == {| class="wikitable" width="100%" |- !家系 !氏名 !官位など !処罰内容 |- |[[橘氏]] |[[橘奈良麻呂]] |[[正四位|正四位下]]・[[参議]] |獄死 |- |[[皇族]] |[[安宿王]] |正四位下・[[讃岐国#国司|讃岐守]] |妻子とともに[[佐渡国]]へ流罪 |- |皇族 |[[黄文王]] |[[従四位|従四位上]]・[[散位寮|散位頭]] |久奈多夫礼と改名の上、獄死 |- |皇族 |[[道祖王]] |元[[皇太子]] |麻度比と改名の上、獄死 |- |[[藤原南家]] |[[藤原豊成]] |[[正二位]]・[[右大臣]] |[[大宰帥|大宰員外帥]]に左遷{{Efn|ただし赴任せず。抗議として難波の別荘に籠って政情が変わるまで8年間過ごした。仲麻呂失脚時に右大臣として復権。}} |- |藤原南家 |[[藤原乙縄]] |[[正六位|正六位上]] |[[日向国#国司|日向員外掾]]に左遷{{Efn|仲麻呂失脚後、復権。}} |- |[[多治比氏]] |[[多治比広足]] |[[従三位]]・[[中納言]] |中納言を解任 |- |多治比氏 |[[多治比国人]] |従四位下・[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]] |[[伊豆国]]へ流罪 |- |多治比氏 |[[多治比犢養]] |従五位上 |獄死 |- |多治比氏 |多治比礼麻呂 | |獄死？ |- |多治比氏 |多治比鷹主 | |獄死？ |- |[[大伴氏]] |[[大伴古麻呂]] |正四位下・[[弁官|左大弁]] |獄死 |- |大伴氏 |[[大伴古慈斐]] |従四位上・[[土佐国#国司|土佐守]] |任地の[[土佐国]]で解官。そのまま土佐に流罪{{Efn|仲麻呂失脚後、復権。}} |- |大伴氏 |[[大伴駿河麻呂]] |従五位下 |流罪？{{Efn|光仁朝にて復権}} |- |大伴氏 |[[大伴池主]] |[[式部省|式部少丞]] |獄死？ |- |大伴氏 |大伴兄人 | |獄死？ |- |[[佐伯氏]] |[[佐伯全成]] |[[従五位|従五位上]]・[[陸奥国司|陸奥守]] |自害 |- |佐伯氏 |[[佐伯大成 (公家)|佐伯大成]] |従五位下・[[信濃国#国司|信濃守]] |[[信濃国]]へ流罪 |- |[[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏]] |[[賀茂角足]] |[[正五位|正五位上]]・[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]] |乃呂志と改名の上、獄死 |- |[[小野氏]] |[[小野東人]] |従五位上・前[[備前国#国司|備前守]] |獄死 |- |その他 |[[答本忠節]] |外従五位下 |獄死？ |- |その他 |山田三井比売嶋 |従五位下（故人） |山田三井宿禰から山田史へ改姓 |- |}

== 参考文献 == * 北山茂夫「天平末葉における橘奈良麻呂の変」『立命館法学』2号、1952年。 * 中川幸広「橘奈良麻呂の変」『古代史を彩る万葉の人々』笠間書院、1975年。 * 菊池克美「橘奈良麻呂の変」『別冊歴史読本』23巻6号、1998年。 * 木本好信「橘奈良麻呂の変」『奈良時代の人びとと政争』おうふう、2003年 * 木本好信「橘諸兄と橘奈良麻呂の変」『奈良平安時代史の諸問題』和泉書房、2021年 * 木本好信「黄文王と橘奈良麻呂」『奈良平安時代史の諸問題』和泉書房、2021年（原論文:『史聚』53号、2020年） *『続日本紀』3 新日本古典文学大系14 岩波書店、1992年 * 宇治谷孟訳『続日本紀』全現代語訳（中）、講談社学術文庫、1992年 * [[青木和夫]]『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』[[中央公論社]]、1965年
{{デフォルトソート:たちはなのならまろのらん}} [[Category:奈良時代の事件]] [[Category:天皇史]] [[Category:757年の日本]] [[Category:日本におけるクーデター]] [[Category:孝謙天皇]] [[Category:藤原仲麻呂]]

'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmū|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In [[743]] ({{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野東人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.

<!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion-->

'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmū|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In [[743]] ({{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野東人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}
{{translated page|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|version=101607497|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Kōmyō|qq=}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nakamaro|qq=}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|qq=}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōsai|qq=}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|qq=}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|qq=}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|qq=}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Junnin|qq=}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi|qq=}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}
