revision.text:
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Moroe|qq=}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmū|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōmū|qq=}}.
In [[743]] ({{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō|qq=}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|qq=}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野東人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|qq=}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|qq=}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Kōmyō|qq=}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nakamaro|qq=}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|qq=}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōsai|qq=}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|qq=}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|qq=}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|qq=}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Junnin|qq=}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi|qq=}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Empress Kōken]]
revision.text:
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Moroe|qq=}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmū|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōmū|qq=}}.
In [[743]] ({{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō|qq=}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|qq=}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野東人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|qq=}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|qq=}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=}}. {{ill|Empress Dowager Kōmyō|ja|光明皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Kōmyō|qq=}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nakamaro|qq=}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|qq=}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijosai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōsai|qq=}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|qq=}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|qq=}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|qq=}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Junnin|qq=}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi|qq=}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Junnin|qq=}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Taishi|ja|太師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi|qq=}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|[[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]]] [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], held political power during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmū]].
In [[743]] ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|qq=}}, conspiring with [[:en:Ono no Azumabito|Ono no Azumabito]] and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|qq=}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|qq=}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|qq=}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|qq=}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|qq=}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:奈良時代の事件
revision.text:
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|[[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]]] [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], held political power during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmū]].
In [[743]] ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|qq=}}, conspiring with [[:en:Ono no Azumabito|Ono no Azumabito]] and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|qq=}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|qq=}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|qq=}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|qq=}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|qq=}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:藤原仲麻呂
revision.text:
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|[[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]]] [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], held political power during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmū]].
In [[743]] ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|qq=}}, conspiring with [[:en:Ono no Azumabito|Ono no Azumabito]] and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|qq=}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|qq=}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|qq=}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|qq=}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|qq=}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

revision.text:
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|[[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]]] [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], held political power during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmū]].
In [[743]] ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|qq=}}, conspiring with [[:en:Ono no Azumabito|Ono no Azumabito]] and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|qq=}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|qq=}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|qq=}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|qq=}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|qq=}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|qq=}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

<!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--> [[Category:757 in Japan�]] [[Category:Japanese imperial history]] [[Category:奈良時代の事件]] [[Category:Empress Kōken]] [[Category:Military coups in Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]]

'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|[[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]]] [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], held political power during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmū]].
In [[743]] ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|qq=}}, conspiring with [[:en:Ono no Azumabito|Ono no Azumabito]] and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|qq=}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|qq=}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|qq=}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|qq=}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|qq=}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.

Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.

In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.

In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|qq=}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|qq=}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|qq=}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|[[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]]] [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], held political power during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmū]].
In [[:en:743|743]] ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|qq=}}, conspiring with [[:en:Ono no Azumabito|Ono no Azumabito]] and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[:en:738|738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|qq=}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|qq=}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|qq=}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|qq=}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|qq=}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|qq=}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.

On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|qq=}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|qq=}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.

Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
