Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540090}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|1=Yoshinobu Kimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518614}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|1=Shioyaki-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428805}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇|1=Junnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329713}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞|1=Fujiwara no Otosada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623249}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|1=Asakabe-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450566}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[:en:758|758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[:en:764|764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.

<!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--> [[Category:757 in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese imperial history]] [[Category:Incidents of the Nara period]] [[Category:Empress Kōken]] [[Category:Military coups in Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] {{wikidata link|Q11544227}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11544227]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Naramaro's Rebellion [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[:en:Tachibana no Naramaro|Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|[[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]]] [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], held political power during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmū]].
In [[:en:743|743]] ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|1=Saeki no Matanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382793}}, conspiring with [[:en:Ono no Azumabito|Ono no Azumabito]] and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[:en:738|738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[:en:749|749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|1=Shibi Chūdai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605723}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[:en:755|755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|1=Sami no Miyamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933252}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|1=Saeki no Minomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95512472}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[:en:756|756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|1=Funado-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641786}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[:en:757|757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540090}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|1=Yoshinobu Kimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518614}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|1=Shioyaki-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428805}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇|1=Junnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329713}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞|1=Fujiwara no Otosada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623249}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|1=Asakabe-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450566}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. In [[:en:758|758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[:en:764|764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11544227]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Naramaro's Rebellion [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[:en:Tachibana no Naramaro|Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|[[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]]] [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], held political power during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmū]].
In [[:en:743|743]] ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|1=Saeki no Matanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382793}}, conspiring with [[:en:Ono no Azumabito|Ono no Azumabito]] and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[:en:738|738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[:en:749|749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|1=Shibi Chūdai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605723}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[:en:755|755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|1=Sami no Miyamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933252}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|1=Saeki no Minomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95512472}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[:en:756|756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|1=Funado-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641786}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[:en:757|757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540090}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|1=Yoshinobu Kimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518614}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|1=Shioyaki-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428805}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇|1=Junnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329713}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞|1=Fujiwara no Otosada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623249}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|1=Asakabe-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450566}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[:en:758|758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[:en:764|764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}
{{translated page|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|version=101607497|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[:en:755|755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|1=Sami no Miyamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933252}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|1=Saeki no Minomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95512472}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[:en:756|756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|1=Funado-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641786}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[:en:757|757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540090}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|1=Yoshinobu Kimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518614}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|1=Shioyaki-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428805}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇|1=Junnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329713}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞|1=Fujiwara no Otosada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623249}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|1=Asakabe-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450566}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[:en:758|758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[:en:764|764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.

In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[:en:Tachibana no Naramaro|Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|[[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]]] [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], held political power during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmū]].
In [[:en:743|743]] ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|1=Saeki no Matanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382793}}, conspiring with [[:en:Ono no Azumabito|Ono no Azumabito]] and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[:en:738|738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[:en:749|749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|1=Shibi Chūdai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605723}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[:en:755|755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|1=Sami no Miyamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933252}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[:en:756|756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} but failed. === Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[:en:758|758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[:en:764|764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.

<!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--> [[Category:757 in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese imperial history]] [[Category:Incidents of the Nara period]] [[Category:Empress Kōken]] [[Category:Military coups in Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] {{wikidata link|Q11544227}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11544227]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Naramaro's Rebellion [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[:en:Tachibana no Naramaro|Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|[[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]]] [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], held political power during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmū]].
In [[:en:743|743]] ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|1=Saeki no Matanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382793}}, conspiring with [[:en:Ono no Azumabito|Ono no Azumabito]] and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[:en:738|738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[:en:749|749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|1=Shibi Chūdai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605723}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[:en:755|755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|1=Sami no Miyamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933252}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|1=Saeki no Minomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95512472}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[:en:756|756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|1=Funado-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641786}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[:en:757|757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540090}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|1=Yoshinobu Kimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518614}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|1=Shioyaki-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428805}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇|1=Junnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329713}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞|1=Fujiwara no Otosada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623249}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|1=Asakabe-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450566}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[:en:758|758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[:en:764|764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.

In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[:en:Tachibana no Naramaro|Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.

The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[:en:749|749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|1=Shibi Chūdai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605723}}. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[:en:755|755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|1=Sami no Miyamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933252}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|1=Saeki no Minomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95512472}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[:en:756|756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|1=Funado-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641786}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[:en:757|757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540090}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|1=Yoshinobu Kimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518614}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|1=Shioyaki-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428805}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇|1=Junnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329713}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.

However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.

In [[:en:758|758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[:en:764|764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.

In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), [[:en:Tachibana no Naramaro|Tachibana no Naramaro]] conspired to overthrow [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|[[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]]] [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], held political power during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmū]].
In [[:en:743|743]] ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|1=Saeki no Matanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382793}}, conspiring with [[:en:Ono no Azumabito|Ono no Azumabito]] and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in [[:en:738|738]] (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In [[:en:749|749]] (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]. [[:en:Empress Kōmyō|Empress Dowager Kōmyō]] trusted [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|1=Shibi Chūdai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605723}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In [[:en:755|755]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|1=Sami no Miyamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933252}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|1=Saeki no Minomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95512472}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February [[:en:756|756]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|1=Funado-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641786}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January [[:en:757|757]] (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540090}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|1=Yoshinobu Kimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518614}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|1=Shioyaki-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428805}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇|1=Junnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329713}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞|1=Fujiwara no Otosada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623249}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|1=Asakabe-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450566}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[:en:758|758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[:en:764|764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540090}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|1=Yoshinobu Kimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518614}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|1=Shioyaki-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428805}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇|1=Junnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329713}} (later [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]]), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞|1=Fujiwara no Otosada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623249}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|1=Asakabe-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450566}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In [[:en:758|758]] (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to [[:en:Grand Preceptor|Taishi]] (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By [[:en:764|764]] (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11544227]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Naramaro's Rebellion [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro|en|Tachibana no Naramaro|WD=Q7673717|lt=Tachibana no Naramaro}} conspired to overthrow {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}} and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmū}}.
In {{ill|743|en|743|WD=Q23604|lt=743}} ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|1=Saeki no Matanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382793}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|en|Ono no Azumabito|WD=Q3448019|lt=Ono no Azumabito}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in {{ill|738|en|738|WD=Q23585|lt=738}} (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In {{ill|749|en|749|WD=Q23624|lt=749}} (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken}}. {{ill|Empress Kōmyō|en|Empress Kōmyō|WD=Q837932|lt=Empress Dowager Kōmyō}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|1=Shibi Chūdai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605723}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In {{ill|755|en|755|WD=Q23650|lt=755}} (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|1=Sami no Miyamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933252}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|1=Saeki no Minomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95512472}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February {{ill|756|en|756|WD=Q31121|lt=756}} (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|1=Funado-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641786}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January {{ill|757|en|757|WD=Q409717|lt=757}} (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540090}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|1=Yoshinobu Kimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518614}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|1=Shioyaki-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428805}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇|1=Junnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329713}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞|1=Fujiwara no Otosada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623249}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|1=Asakabe-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450566}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In {{ill|758|en|758|WD=Q30538|lt=758}} (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Grand Preceptor|en|Grand Preceptor|WD=Q836410|lt=Taishi}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By {{ill|764|en|764|WD=Q31163|lt=764}} (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞|1=Fujiwara no Otosada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623249}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|1=Asakabe-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450566}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In {{ill|758|en|758|WD=Q30538|lt=758}} (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Grand Preceptor|en|Grand Preceptor|WD=Q836410|lt=Taishi}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By {{ill|764|en|764|WD=Q31163|lt=764}} (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

<!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--><!--Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] {{wikidata link|Q11544227}} [[Category:757 in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Empress Kōken]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Nakamaro]] [[Category:Incidents of the Nara period]] [[Category:Japanese imperial history]] [[Category:Military coups in Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11544227]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Naramaro's Rebellion [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro|en|Tachibana no Naramaro|WD=Q7673717|lt=Tachibana no Naramaro}} conspired to overthrow {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}} and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmū}}.
In {{ill|743|en|743|WD=Q23604|lt=743}} ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|1=Saeki no Matanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382793}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|en|Ono no Azumabito|WD=Q3448019|lt=Ono no Azumabito}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in {{ill|738|en|738|WD=Q23585|lt=738}} (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In {{ill|749|en|749|WD=Q23624|lt=749}} (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken}}. {{ill|Empress Kōmyō|en|Empress Kōmyō|WD=Q837932|lt=Empress Dowager Kōmyō}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|1=Shibi Chūdai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605723}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In {{ill|755|en|755|WD=Q23650|lt=755}} (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|1=Sami no Miyamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933252}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|1=Saeki no Minomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95512472}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February {{ill|756|en|756|WD=Q31121|lt=756}} (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|1=Funado-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641786}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January {{ill|757|en|757|WD=Q409717|lt=757}} (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540090}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|1=Yoshinobu Kimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518614}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|1=Shioyaki-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428805}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇|1=Junnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329713}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞|1=Fujiwara no Otosada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623249}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|1=Asakabe-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450566}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In {{ill|758|en|758|WD=Q30538|lt=758}} (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Grand Preceptor|en|Grand Preceptor|WD=Q836410|lt=Taishi}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By {{ill|764|en|764|WD=Q31163|lt=764}} (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.

However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By {{ill|764|en|764|WD=Q31163|lt=764}} (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:橘奈良麻呂の乱]][[ko:다치바나노 나라마로의 난]]
'''Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro''' (橘奈良麻呂の乱, Tachibana no Naramaro no Ran) was a political coup attempt during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. In the ninth year of [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] (757 CE), {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro|en|Tachibana no Naramaro|WD=Q7673717|lt=Tachibana no Naramaro}} conspired to overthrow {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}} and plotted to depose the emperor, but the scheme was discovered through betrayal and ended in failure.
== Summary == === Preceding Events === The father of Tachibana Naramaro, [[Left Minister|{{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}}]] {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}, held political power during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmū}}.
In {{ill|743|en|743|WD=Q23604|lt=743}} ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 15), during Emperor Shōmū’s illness while staying in Naniwa, Naramaro revealed plans to {{ill|Saeki Zenjō|ja|佐伯全成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Zenjō|1=Saeki no Matanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382793}}, conspiring with {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|en|Ono no Azumabito|WD=Q3448019|lt=Ono no Azumabito}} and others to su11ort {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}} as the next emperor. Although Princess {{ill|Abe no Naishinnō|ja|阿倍内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Naishinnō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} had already been a11ointed crown princess in {{ill|738|en|738|WD=Q23585|lt=738}} (Tenpyō 10), Naramaro claimed there was no established successor, as female emperors were single (either unmarried or widowed) and limited to a single reign. The move reflected an underlying desire for a male heir.
In {{ill|749|en|749|WD=Q23624|lt=749}} (Tenpyō 21/Tenpyō Kanpō 1/Tenpyō Shōhō 1), Emperor Shōmū abdicated, and Abe no Naishinnō ascended as {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken}}. {{ill|Empress Kōmyō|en|Empress Kōmyō|WD=Q837932|lt=Empress Dowager Kōmyō}} trusted {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, a11ointing him head of the newly established {{ill|Shibi Chūdai|ja|紫微中台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibi Chūdai|1=Shibi Chūdai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605723}}. Nakamaro, favored by Empress Kōken, quickly rose in power, while Naramaro’s family influence waned. In November, during the enthronement {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai}}, Naramaro again conspired with Saeki Zenjō but failed to execute the plan due to Zenjō’s refusal.
In {{ill|755|en|755|WD=Q23650|lt=755}} (Tenpyō Shōhō 7), {{ill|Sami no Miyamori|ja|佐味宮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Miyamori|1=Sami no Miyamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933252}} accused Moroe of criticizing the court during a feast. Although Emperor Shōmū dismissed the issue, {{ill|Saeki Minomaro|ja|佐伯美濃麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Minomaro|1=Saeki no Minomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95512472}} was questioned as a witness. When Minomaro redirected attention to Zenjō, the matter ended with Empress Dowager Kōmyō’s intervention.
In February {{ill|756|en|756|WD=Q31121|lt=756}} (Tenpyō Shōhō 8), Moroe resigned, humiliated. In April, during Emperor Shōmū’s final illness, Naramaro attempted to conspire once again, involving {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} but failed. On May 2, Emperor Shōmū passed away, and {{ill|Prince Funado|ja|道祖王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Funado|1=Funado-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641786}} was named crown prince per his will.
In January {{ill|757|en|757|WD=Q409717|lt=757}} (Tenpyō Shōhō 9), Moroe died at 74. Scholars like {{ill|Morita Tei|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tei|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540090}} and {{ill|Nakamura Junshō|ja|中村順昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Junshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} suggest Moroe o11osed Naramaro’s plans or was unaware of them, while {{ill|Kimoto Yoshinobu|ja|木本好信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Yoshinobu|1=Yoshinobu Kimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518614}} argues Moroe may have been indirectly involved.
In March, Prince Funado fell out of favor and was deposed. In April, deliberations on the next crown prince led to {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|ja|塩焼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtsu|1=Shioyaki-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428805}} being rejected. Instead, {{ill|Prince Ōī|ja|大炊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōī|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淳仁天皇|1=Junnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329713}} (later {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}), su11orted by Nakamaro, was chosen.
=== The Conspiracy and Its Discovery === Dissatisfied with Nakamaro’s dominance, Naramaro gathered like-minded individuals to plot his removal{{Efn|Nakamaro, aware of Naramaro’s movements, preemptively transferred {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}} to Mutsu Province on June 16.}}. On {{ill|June 28|6月28日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 28|qq=}} (July 18), {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|ja|山背王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原弟貞|1=Fujiwara no Otosada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623249}} reported the conspiracy to Empress Kōken. The next day, a meeting was held where Naramaro and 20 others, including Komaro, {{ill|Prince Ōbu|ja|黄文王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōbu|1=Kibumi-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677744}}, and {{ill|Prince Anjuku|ja|安宿王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anjuku|1=Asakabe-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450566}}, swore to rebel on {{ill|July 2|7月2日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2|qq=}} (July 22).
On July 2, Empress Kōken and Empress Dowager Kōmyō issued an edict urging loyalty. However, that evening, {{ill|Uemichi no Fiyata|ja|上道斐太都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemichi no Fiyata|1=Uemichi no Fiyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360193}} exposed the plot to Nakamaro. By the next day, Naramaro, Komaro, and others were arrested. Torture extracted confessions of plans to attack Nakamaro’s residence, depose the crown prince, and install a new emperor.
=== Harsh Punishments === On {{ill|July 4|7月4日 (k久治光平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 4|qq=}} (July 24), suspects, including Prince Funado, Prince Ōbu, and Naramaro, were tortured. Many, including Naramaro, died under interrogation. Prince Anjuku was exiled to Sado Island, and others faced demotions or exile. Over 443 individuals were punished.
=== Aftermath === Nakamaro used the incident to consolidate power. In {{ill|758|en|758|WD=Q30538|lt=758}} (Tenpyō Hōji 2), Prince Ōī ascended as Emperor Junnin, elevating Nakamaro to {{ill|Grand Preceptor|en|Grand Preceptor|WD=Q836410|lt=Taishi}} (Grand Minister). However, Nakamaro’s fall came swiftly. By {{ill|764|en|764|WD=Q31163|lt=764}} (Tenpyō Hōji 8), he rebelled and was defeated, marking the end of his dominance.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Kitayama|title=Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|publisher=Ritsumeikan Law Journal|year=1952}} * {{Cite book|author=Yukihiro Nakagawa|title=Ancient Japanese History: Man'yō People|publisher=Kasama Publishing|year=1975}} * {{Cite book|author=Katsumi Kikuchi|title=The Rebellion of Tachibana no Naramaro|publisher=Historical Reader|year=1998}} * {{Cite book|author=Yoshinobu Kimoto|title=Nara Period Figures and Conflicts|publisher=Ofu|year=2003}} * {{Cite book|author=Masanori Ueno|title=The Inner Politics of the Nara Court|publisher=Yagi Books|year=2023}}
